test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: For certain air temperatures, the table gives wind chill temperatures for two different wind speeds. According to the table, what is the wind chill temperature, in degrees Fahrenheit (°F), when the air temperature is 38°F and the wind speed is 60 miles per hour (mph)? (Disregard the degree symbol when entering your answer.)

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: 4m = 3(n + p) The given equation relates the positive numbers m, n, and p. Which equation correctly gives m in terms of n and p?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: Line u is defined by y = 2x + 10. Line w is perpendicular to line u in the xy -plane. What is the slope of line w?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: For the linear function f, the graph of y = f(x) in the xy -plane has a slope of $\frac{1}{3}$ and passes through the point (0, 2). Which equation defines f?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: During a trip, Bill drove his car for 16.0 miles and then began driving at a constant speed of 60.0 miles per hour. After 2 hours of driving at this speed, what is the total distance, in miles, Bill has traveled during this trip?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: 3x + 10 = 2(7 + 4x) Which of the following is equivalent to the given equation?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: In triangle JKL, the measures of ∠K and ∠L are each 34°. What is the measure of ∠J, in degrees? (Disregard the degree symbol when entering your answer.)

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: x + 4 = 8 (x + 4) 2 = y Which ordered pair (x, y) is a solution to the given system of equations?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: Nicole walks at a speed of 2 miles per hour and runs at a speed of 7 miles per hour. She walks for w hours and runs for r hours for a combined total of 13 miles. Which equation represents this situation?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: The value of z is 1.12 times 100. The value of z is what percent greater than 100?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: Circle K has a radius of 2 millimeters (mm). Circle L has an area of 121π mm 2. What is the total area, in mm 2, of circles K and L?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: If 3 x + 4 = 10, what is the value of 9 x − 4?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: One leg of a right triangle has a length of 43.2 millimeters. The hypotenuse of the triangle has a length of 196.8 millimeters. What is the length of the other leg of the triangle, in millimeters?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: What is the sum of the solutions to the equation 2x 2 − 6x + 5 = 13?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: y = 23 y = −3(x − 23) 2 + 20 If the given equations are graphed in the xy -plane, at how many points do the graphs of the equations intersect?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: y = 400(3) 2x The equation gives the estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of every six-month period, x, starting from the end of January 1997, where 0 ≤ x ≤ 4. Which statement is the best interpretation of the y-intercept of the graph of this equation in the xy -plane?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of the first six-month period was 400.

choices.label: B
choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1997 was 400.

choices.label: C
choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1998 was 1,200.

choices.label: D
choices.text: The estimated number of online newsletter subscribers at the end of January 1997 was 1,200.

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: The function f is defined by f(x) = (−2)(3) x + 21. What is the y -intercept of the graph of y = f(x) in the xy -plane?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: 5x + 14y = 32 10x + 7y = 22 The solution to the given system of equations is (x, y). What is the value of xy?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: A scientist initially measures 12,000 bacteria in a growth medium. 5 hours later, the scientist measures 24,000 bacteria. Assuming exponential growth, the formula P = C(2) rt gives the number of bacteria in the growth medium, where r and C are constants and P is the number of bacteria t hours after the initial measurement. What is the value of r?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: Two variables, x and y, are related such that for each increase of 1 in the value of x, the value of y increases by a factor of 3. When x = 0, y = 200. Which equation represents this relationship?

passage: The shaded region shown represents the solutions to which inequality?

test_name: May 2024 International Form C SAT Math Module 1
passage: 2|6x − 1|= 6x + 9 What is the sum of the solutions to the given equation?
